steps.title: Use supplementary angles from parallel lines
steps.content: When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, all 4 acute angles are equal to each other, and all 4 obtuse angles are equal to each other. Any acute angle and any obtuse angle form a linear pair and are supplementary: acute + obtuse = 180°.

steps.title: Express the obtuse angle in terms of x
steps.content:
One acute angle measures $(7x - 410)°$. Since it is supplementary to each obtuse angle:

$$180 - (7x - 410) = 590 - 7x$$

So each obtuse angle measures $(590 - 7x)°$.

steps.title: Calculate the sum to find w
steps.content:
Sum of one acute angle and three obtuse angles:

$$(7x - 410) + 3(590 - 7x)$$
$$= 7x - 410 + 1770 - 21x$$
$$= -14x + 1360$$

Matching this to $(-14x + w)°$, we find $w = 1360$.
